what: Pin upstream versions in issue/PR bodies
title: link
text: issue PR body describes upstream behaviour (bcfp SQL, fresh primitives, fwapg, etc.) rationale reference, pin upstream version. Use <owner>/<repo>@<version--sha> format: bcfp: deterministic ref bcfishpass.log.model_version (e.g. smnorris/bcfishpass@v0.7.14-125-g6e9cf1c). Cite model_run_id + date alongside human readability. fresh / link / NGE: tag refs (fresh@v0.29.0) short SHAs (fresh@f42e86a). fwapg / db_newgraph: — tag short SHA. Without version pin, “behaviour exists upstream” claims rot — six-month-old issues end describing code longer exists, one knows compared . Version-pinning makes issue self-contained reproducible. Note: <owner>/<repo>@<sha> references commit; trigger GitHub notifications referenced repo’s participants (unlike <owner>/<repo>#<n> issue/PR references — see feedback_no_cross_ref_external_issues.md memory).

title: link
text: Six-phase bcfishpass-reproducing pipeline, driven lnk_config() + lnk_load_overrides(). Every phase reads data table takes cfg (manifest) loaded (named list lnk_load_overrides()). Callers materialize thread loaded . - lnk_pipeline_run(conn, aoi, cfg, loaded, schema, dams, cleanup_working, mapping_code) — modelling umbrella; chains phases plus lnk_persist_init + lnk_barriers_unify + lnk_pipeline_persist one per-WSG call. Writes <persist_schema>.streams, streams_habitat_<sp>, barriers. mapping_code = TRUE additionally writes streams_access + streams_mapping_code (tunnel-free; v0.40.0). modelling boundary — comparison separate (lnk_compare_rollup, lnk_compare_wsg). - lnk_pipeline_setup(conn, schema, overwrite) — create per-run working schema. - lnk_pipeline_load(conn, aoi, cfg, loaded, schema) — crossings + modelled fixes + PSCIS status overrides. Reads loaded$user_modelled_crossing_fixes, loaded$user_pscis_barrier_status, loaded$user_crossings_misc. - lnk_pipeline_prepare(conn, aoi, cfg, loaded, schema) — falls, definite + control, habitat confirms, gradient barriers, natural_barriers, barrier overrides, per-model minimal reduction, base segments. Manifest-key gating via loaded$user_barriers_definite_control loaded$user_habitat_classification (DB probes). - lnk_pipeline_break(conn, aoi, cfg, loaded, schema) — sequential frs_break_apply config-defined order: observations → gradient minimal → barriers_definite (separate break source) → habitat endpoints → crossings. - lnk_pipeline_classify(conn, aoi, cfg, loaded, schema) — assembles fresh.streams_breaks (gradient FULL + falls + barriers_definite + crossings, WSG-filtered) runs frs_habitat_classify(). barriers_definite enters directly bcfishpass appends user-definite post-filter (via observation override). - lnk_pipeline_connect(conn, aoi, cfg, loaded, schema) — per-species cluster + connected_waterbody. - lnk_pipeline_species(cfg, loaded, aoi) — canonical helper “species config classifies AOI” (intersects cfg$species loaded$wsg_species_presence presence; falls back loaded$parameters_fresh$species_code cfg$species missing).

text: Variables double-quoted strings containing single quotes break value ' "echo '${VAR}'" — VAR contains ', shell syntax breaks Use printf '%s\n' "$VAR" | command pipe values safely Heredocs: unquoted <<EOF expands variables locally, <<'EOF' — know need Pass--ssh args: printf '%q' escapes per-arg workload paths spaces / quotes / metacharacters survive local-shell → ssh-argv → remote-shell round-trip. Without , ssh host 'cmd' "$path" joins args spaces remote re-parses, losing argument boundaries.

title: link
text: cmd1 | cmd2 <<EOF — heredoc binds cmd2 (rightmost simple command). intended cmd1 receive , put <<EOF cmd1 explicitly: cmd1 <<EOF | cmd2. Symptom wrong: ssh body silently echoed tee/cat/etc, ssh side gets empty stdin, exits 0 (near-0) without anything. Caught hard way 2026-05-01 cypher_restore-fwapg.sh.

title: link
text: set -eu propagate exit codes pipelines. ssh ... | tee log returns tee’s exit (always 0 healthy tee), masking ssh failure. Use set -euo pipefail script pipes meaningful command tee/cat/grep/etc. check ${PIPESTATUS[0]} explicitly. Symptom wrong: task notifications report “exit 0 / completed” remote work actually skipped errored.

text: runcmd item containing { : risk parsed YAML flow-mapping (dict), literal string. Cloud-init’s shellify hits non-string throws TypeError, aborting subsequent runcmd steps silently final_message still fires. Don’t write: - test -s /file || { echo "FATAL: ..." } — : inside braces makes YAML see dict. write: use - | block scalar explicit //fi: Validate post-edit: python3 -c "import yaml; runcmd=yaml.safe_load(open('cloud-init.yaml').read().split(chr(10),1)[1])['runcmd']; print([type(x).__name__ x runcmd isinstance(x,str)] 'strings')". output anything strings, runcmd fail.
code: - |   if [ ! -s /file ]; then     echo "FATAL: ..." >&2     exit 1   fi

Sizes vs disks writing: g-4vcpu-16gb = 50 GB, g-8vcpu-32gb / m-4vcpu-32gb = 100 GB, m-8vcpu-64gb = 200 GB. workload requires X GB RAM minimum, snapshot floor whatever droplet X GB smallest disk class.

text: tidyverse style guide: snake_case, pipe operators (|> %>%) Match existing patterns codebase Use pak package installation (install.packages) Prefix column name vectors cols_ discoverability environment pane: cols_all, cols_carry, cols_split, cols_writable. principle grouped vectors (params_, tbl_, etc.) Function parameters name database table use table_<role>: table_in, table_out, table_to, table_pscis, table_modelled, table_target. Picked <role>_table (e.g. pscis_table) consistency group table args together autocomplete / signature views. Also picked bare destination tables — table_to explicit ’s passed. Single-noun args stay role name (e.g. segments, observations, crossings). Existing functions using <role>_table bare migrate opportunistically touched; big-bang rename. convention column-name parameters: col_<role>: col_a_id, col_b_id, col_segment_id, col_blue_line_key, col_key. Picked <role>_col (e.g. segment_id_col) autocomplete-grouping reason. fresh’s existing segment_id_col / feature_id_col precede convention migrate opportunistically. convention SQL-expression parameters: exp_<role>: exp_score, exp_filter, exp_where, exp_select. SQL fragments caller writes get embedded generated query. Like table_* col_*, exp_ prefix groups expression args together autocomplete / signature views. SQL DDL+INSERT pairs share schema, use single named vector source truth. CREATE TABLE INSERT (cols) SELECT cols derive column lists cols_* vector. Avoids drift table shape write projection — columns change, edit one place. Example:
code: cols_streams <- c(   id_segment           = "integer NOT NULL",   watershed_group_code = "varchar(4) NOT NULL",   geom                 = "geometry(MultiLineStringZM, 3005)"   # … ) # CREATE TABLE consumes both names + types ddl_body <- paste(names(cols_streams), unname(cols_streams), sep = " ",                   collapse = ", ") # INSERT consumes names only proj <- paste(names(cols_streams), collapse = ", ")

text: Computes <schema>.streams_access — per-segment, per-species accessibility plus downstream barrier-source flags. Two distinct inputs, two distinct roles: barriers_per_sp — named list sp → barriers_<sp>_access (#200; _unified pre-v0.40.4). Drives has_barriers_<sp>_dnstr (blocking natural barrier downstream species, override-applied). accessibility — feeds accessible mapping_code. NATURAL-+ override-filtered + user_definite (§5) — dams . table’s feature id derived <table>_id passed frs_network_features. barrier_sources — named list source-typed feature tables (anthropogenic, pscis, dams, remediations). Drives has_barriers_<source>_dnstr / dam_dnstr_ind / remediated_dnstr_ind flags. classification (kind barrier downstream), accessibility. Feeds token2 (DAM/MODELLED/ASSESSED/…). output flag columns (has_barriers_{anthropogenic,pscis,dams,remediations}_dnstr, dam_dnstr_ind, remediated_dnstr_ind) MUST persisted — see §6 gotcha.

text: Token format: {ACCESS|SPAWN|REAR|""};{NONE|DAM|MODELLED|ASSESSED|REMEDIATED}[;INTERMITTENT] species, per segment (lnk_pipeline_mapping_code.R:~196-289): Note asymmetry: SPAWN/REAR fire habitat presence regardless accessibility, token2 (barrier descriptor) suppressed segment inaccessible. no_data (NA has_barriers_<sp>_dnstr) → emit "". Species absent WSG (via presence) → emit "" rows.
code: accessible = !has_barriers_<sp>_dnstr  &  has_data        # from barriers_per_sp  token1 (non-spawn-only):   ACCESS  if accessible AND spawning==0 AND rearing==0     # accessible, no habitat   SPAWN   elif spawning > 0                                # ← fires regardless of access   REAR    elif spawning==0 AND rearing > 0                 # ← fires regardless of access   else NA  token2 = ifelse(accessible, mc_barrier, NA)                # ← GATED on accessible token3 = ifelse(accessible & intermittent, "INTERMITTENT", NA)  mc_barrier (from barrier_sources flags, resident vs anadromous differ slightly):   REMEDIATED if remediated_dnstr   DAM        elif dam_dnstr   ASSESSED   elif anthropogenic & pscis        (resident) / elif pscis (anadr)   MODELLED   elif anthropogenic                (no pscis)   NONE       elif no anthropogenic

text: Two senses, keep apart: access_gradient_max → gradient class per species (salmon 0.15, BT 0.25). observation_threshold / observation_date_min / observation_species → override. match bcfp exactly: BT row = threshold 1, date 1900, species BT;CH;CO;SK;PK;CM;ST (bcfp: ≥1 obs, BT+salmon+steelhead, “passable salmon ⟹ passable BT”); CH row = threshold 5, date 1990, species CH;CM;CO;PK;SK (bcfp: >5 obs since 1990). rules exist species-specific; ’re just wired lnk_pipeline_access yet. Descriptor (token2): YES, species-class-specific already link — resident (mcbi_r: next-downstream-dam, sequence-aware via dam_dnstr_ind) vs anadromous (mcbi_a: dam downstream via barriers_dams_dnstr). Extend-vs-reproduce fork — “dam override”: many CABD dams exist paper passable (decommissioned, partial, fishway-equipped, fish demonstrably ). general version — let dam overridden relevant set evidence — reuse existing override machinery (lnk_barrier_overrides: observations / confirmed habitat / control), bespoke fishway-passability model. CABD passability_status mapping already drops Passable dams (barrier_status='PASSABLE'); “dam override” extends evidence-based rules rest. Call dam-override (situation varies — fishway just one case; name shouldn’t bake mechanism). departure bcfp (bcfp keeps dams descriptors never overrides per-species) opt-axis — breaks exact-reproduction bar (CLAUDE.md), decide deliberately: match bcfp first (wiring fix ), layer dam-override rules engine.

what: 6. Gotchas that have cost real time
title: link RUNBOOK — how the system actually works
text: Persist column changes matched pair. Adding column streams_access / streams_mapping_code means editing two independently constructed sites: CREATE TABLE DDL lnk_persist_init.R INSERT projection lnk_pipeline_persist.R. DDL column make INSERT populate — don’t share projection. Missing source-flag generator INSERT v0.40.3 NONE-token bug. Verify DDL + INSERT together live data. Tunnel-free build, tunnel-diff. build (pipeline_run + mapping_code) needs tunnel — gradient/falls local, cross-WSG dams come persist. bcfp tunnel (localhost:63333) needed parity diff, ’s flaky. Prefer local snapshot (fresh.streams_vw_bcfp) live tunnel comparison — ’s tunnel-free reproducible. tunnel retired. Redo snapshot weekly. bcfp rebuilds Tuesdays (bcfishpass.log → model_run_id, model_version). bash data-raw/snapshot_bcfp.sh ---bcfp-views --force (PG* env → local docker fwapg) refreshes link’s inputs fresh.*_vw_bcfp comparison. Tunnel-free (public sources: BCDC, CABD, bchamp objectstore, s3://newgraph). snapshot_bcfp.sh ---bcfp-views silently ships streams. bcfishpass.streams_vw.fgb.zip s3 ~1.6 GB won’t stream ogr2ogr /vsizip//vsicurl — gzip read dies mid-file (decompression failed z_err=-1). Worse: ogr2ogr exits 0 premature termination, snapshot’s set -euo pipefail doesn’t catch script reports success crossings_vw_bcfp loaded (small view streams fine). parity-critical streams comparison data just missing. Fix touched: download zip curl first, unzip, ogr2ogr local .fgb; verify row count post-load rather trusting exit code. , streams_vw_bcfp parity needs ~1.6 GB download tunnel. Don’t persist half-built working schema. lnk_pipeline_persist DELETE--WSGs persist tables INSERT — running incomplete working schema wipes good province-wide data WSG. Double-persist wall time. mapping_code = TRUE currently pre-persists barriers (cross-WSG views) persists end → PARS ~16 min vs ~3.5 min normal. Pre-persisting barriers (streams+habitat) open optimization (#196 Phase 5). pkill <R client> cancel Postgres query — backend orphans. Caught 2026-05-25 (link#205): killed recompute left frs_network_features SELECT running 1h45m server-side, holding lock barriers_bt_access; every later lnk_barriers_views DROP VIEW blocked behind indefinitely (silent hangs). R client died; libpq backend . Always terminate server-side backend (SELECT pg_terminate_backend(pid) pg_stat_activity state='active' …), just client. set statement_timeout + lock_timeout long-running DB op (SET statement_timeout = '600000'; SET lock_timeout = '60000') — runaway cancels server-side instead orphaning, blocked DROP VIEW fails fast instead wedging. data-raw/wsg_recompute_one.R sets conn exactly reason. AOI-scoping streams VIEW (real table) makes planner pick wrong join driver. Caught 2026-05-25 (link#205): scoping fresh.streams one WSG via CREATE VIEW … wsg = 'FINA' left Postgres small-table stats; picked ~800k-row barriers_bt_access outer driver frs_network_features’s nested loop, blowing cost ~1000× (estimated 71M result rows, >10 min wall). Solution: materialize real CREATE TABLE id_segment btree + ltree GiST + blue_line_key + ANALYZE. planner picks 26k-row AOI streams outer walk takes ~10s. Mirrors full pipeline’s working schema (also real, indexed table). id_segment (…) cartesian persist schema (link#203). id_segment unique per WSG, globally; query like SELECT * <persist_access> id_segment (SELECT id_segment <streams> wsg = aoi) matches access rows every WSG happens share id_segment values → ~N(WSGs)× duplicates. Filter watershed_group_code directly table column. lnk_mapping_code learned hard way.

title: Compute upstream habitat per crossing — lnk_aggregate
code: # --- "Two barriers are both high severity. #      One blocks 0.3km of rearing habitat. #      The other blocks 12km of spawning habitat for chinook. #      Which do you fix first?" --- if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ conn <- lnk_db_conn()  # Score crossings lnk_score_severity(conn, "working.crossings")  # Compute upstream habitat from fresh output lnk_aggregate(conn,   crossings = "working.crossings",   habitat = "fresh.streams_habitat") # Added spawning_km, rearing_km to working.crossings # Summary: #   spawning_km: min=0.0, median=2.3, max=45.1 #   rearing_km:  min=0.0, median=5.7, max=89.2  # Now you have severity + habitat — the full picture # ORDER BY severity DESC, spawning_km DESC # to find high-severity barriers blocking the most habitat.  # --- Custom metrics --- lnk_aggregate(conn,   crossings = "working.crossings",   habitat = "fresh.streams_habitat",   cols_sum = c(spawning_km = "spawning",                rearing_km = "rearing",                lake_ha = "lake_rearing")) } # }

title: Build barrier override list from evidence sources — lnk_barrier_overrides
text: conn DBI::DBIConnection object. barriers Character. Schema-qualified table barriers columns: blue_line_key, downstream_route_measure, wscode_ltree, localcode_ltree, label. Typically fresh.streams_breaks. observations Character NULL. Schema-qualified table fish observations columns: species_code, blue_line_key, downstream_route_measure, wscode, localcode, observation_date. Typically bcfishobs.observations. habitat Character NULL. Schema-qualified table confirmed habitat canonical shape (post-2026-04-26 bcfishpass): one row per (segment x species) columns species_code, blue_line_key, downstream_route_measure, upstream_route_measure, plus per-habitat-type indicator columns spawning rearing (integer 1 / NULL text 't'/'true'). confirmed habitat (spawning rearing flagged) upstream barrier removes (threshold = 1). exclusions Character NULL. Schema-qualified table observation exclusions column observation_key. Flagged observations removed counting. control Character NULL. Schema-qualified table barrier controls columns: blue_line_key, downstream_route_measure, barrier_ind. Barriers table barrier_ind = TRUE overridden observations — species params$observation_control_apply TRUE. Resident species routinely inhabit reaches upstream anadromous-blocking falls (post-glacial connectivity, ocean-return requirement), observations still count unless flag says otherwise. Habitat confirmations (habitat argument) higher-trust observations — bypass control table entirely, species. params Data frame per-species parameters. Must columns: species_code, observation_threshold, observation_date_min, observation_buffer_m, observation_species. Optional column observation_control_apply (logical) — TRUE, control table blocks overrides species; FALSE/NA/missing, species ignores control. Bcfishpass defaults: TRUE CH/CM/CO/PK/SK/ST, FALSE BT/WCT. See configs/bcfishpass/parameters_fresh.csv. cols_index Character vector. Column names index barriers table fwa_upstream() performance. Indexes created EXISTS. Default c("blue_line_key", "wscode_ltree", "localcode_ltree") — columns exist table indexed. Character. Schema-qualified output table name. verbose Logical. Report counts.

Filters mirror bcfp's model/01_access/sql/barriers_*.sql remediations_barriers.sql: barrier_status ('BARRIER', 'POTENTIAL') anthropogenic-style tables. blue_line_key = watershed_key (excludes side-channel features). barriers_remediations = barriers_anthropogenic UNION crossings pscis_status = 'REMEDIATED' barrier_status = 'PASSABLE' (bcfp-intended logic per v0.30.2 fix; see smnorris/bcfishpass#891).

title: Unify per-WSG barrier sources into the working-schema <schema>.barriers — lnk_barriers_unify
text: Per-WSG output persisted province-wide table lnk_pipeline_persist() using idempotent DELETE--WSG + INSERT pattern already used streams streams_habitat_<sp>. Cross-WSG dam_dnstr_ind resolves correctly fresh::frs_network_features() walks FWA topology care WSG barrier physically lives — fixes PARS BT 60% defect (PARS drains dams PCEA/UPCE WSGs) unblocks regional run. Source families + blocks_species semantics: Anthropogenic (barrier_source ('PSCIS','CABD','MODELLED_CROSSINGS'), <schema>.crossings barrier_status ('BARRIER','POTENTIAL')): blocks 8 species. crossing_source mapped verbatim, keeping MODELLED_CROSSINGS value (vs. lossy normalization MODELLED). Gradient (barrier_source = 'GRADIENT', <schema>.gradient_barriers_raw): blocks species whose access_gradient_max <= gradient_class / 100. Derived per row loaded$parameters_fresh. Falls (barrier_source = 'FALLS', <schema>.falls): blocks 8 species. Subsurface_flow (barrier_source = 'SUBSURFACE_FLOW', <schema>.barriers_subsurfaceflow): blocks 8 species. Opt-(built cfg$pipeline$break_order includes "subsurfaceflow"). User_definite (barrier_source = 'USER_DEFINITE', <schema>.barriers_definite): blocks 8 species. ltrees + linear_feature_id resolved JOIN whse_basemapping.fwa_stream_networks_sp (mirrors FALLS branch bcfp's barriers_user_definite.sql). Persisted province-wide per-species access view (barriers_<sp>_access, lnk_barriers_views()) can include override-exempt — link#200. Remediations (PASSABLE remediation crossings) intentionally table. consumed via <schema>.barriers_remediations (emitted lnk_barriers_emit()) remediated_dnstr_ind sequence-aware logic lnk_pipeline_access(), joins <schema>.crossings directly. id_barrier namespaced per source family rows stay unique inside WSG without coordinating sequence IDs across sources. Mirrors offset trick .lnk_crossings_union uses modelled crossings. Required pre-existing tables schema: <schema>.crossings (lnk_pipeline_crossings()). <schema>.gradient_barriers_raw (lnk_pipeline_prepare()). <schema>.falls (lnk_pipeline_prepare()). <schema>.barriers_definite (lnk_pipeline_prepare(); always created, may empty). Optional: <schema>.barriers_subsurfaceflow (config opts ).

Pass working-schema name (e.g. paste0(schema, ".barriers")) build views per-WSG working table — used lnk_pipeline_run()'s mapping_code phase, runs per-WSG persist write persist barriers may yet hold current data. title: Create working-schema views over <persist_schema>.barriers — lnk_barriers_views
text: views point province-wide table — cross-WSG dnstr walks resolve correctly fresh::frs_network_features() walks FWA topology reads view (unified table). Fixes PARS BT 60% defect (PARS drains dams PCEA / UPCE WSGs) unblocks regional run. Per-species views (two per species bt, ch, cm, co, pk, sk, st, wct): <schema>.barriers_<sp>_unified — filtered '<SP>' = (blocks_species) (barrier families incl dams). _unified suffix avoids name collision per-WSG <schema>.barriers_<sp> tables .lnk_pipeline_prep_minimal builds break-time path. <schema>.barriers_<sp>_access — per-species accessibility set drives accessible mapping_code (link#200). Reproduces bcfp barriers_<sp>: NATURAL barriers (barrier_source ('GRADIENT','FALLS','SUBSURFACE_FLOW') — gradient--species-threshold already encoded blocks_species) MINUS observation/habitat override (anti-join barrier_overrides), ∪ USER_DEFINITE (override-exempt). Dams/PSCIS/modelled excluded — annotate token2 via per-source views, never block access. feature shape (barriers_<sp>_access_id + ltrees + geom) lnk_pipeline_access() / fresh::frs_network_features() consume unchanged. See RUNBOOK.md §5. Per-source views (matching bcfp source-typed tables consumed barrier_sources arg lnk_pipeline_access): <schema>.barriers_anthropogenic_unified — barrier_source ('PSCIS','CABD','MODELLED'). <schema>.barriers_pscis_unified — barrier_source = 'PSCIS'. <schema>.barriers_dams_unified — barrier_source = 'CABD'. (Remediations stay sourced <schema>.barriers_remediations built lnk_barriers_emit() — consumed remediated_dnstr_ind path joins <schema>.crossings directly, via unified barriers table.) Views dropped + recreated call (CREATE REPLACE VIEW) reruns safe. underlying <persist_schema>.barriers table must exist — typically initialized lnk_persist_init() populated lnk_barriers_unify() + lnk_pipeline_persist() WSGs regional scope.

text: conn DBI connection local pipeline database (typically localhost fwapg). aoi Watershed group code (e.g. "ADMS"). cfg lnk_config object (lnk_config()). loaded Named list lnk_load_overrides(). reference Character scalar identifying reference dataset. Currently "bcfishpass" supported. mapping_code Logical. TRUE, run additional barriers_unify → barriers_views → access → mapping_code phases emit per-species segment-match stats. Default FALSE. (Renamed with_mapping_code v0.40.0; old name still accepted deprecation warning v0.41.0.) conn_ref DBI connection reference database. Required reference = "bcfishpass" (bcfp tunnel localhost:63333). Caller manages connection. species Character vector species codes restrict rollup (e.g. c("BT","CH","CM","CO","PK","SK","ST","WCT") 8 bcfp-bundle species). Default NULL uses lnk_pipeline_species() intersected WSG presence. schema Working schema name. Default paste0("working_", tolower(aoi)). dams Logical. TRUE (default), pass conn conn_tunnel lnk_pipeline_prepare() CABD dams step runs local cabd.dams. Pass FALSE skip dams entirely. cleanup_working Logical. TRUE (default), drop <schema> working schema end. Pass FALSE interactive debug / manual inspection. with_mapping_code Deprecated alias mapping_code. Kept one release (v0.40.0); removal v0.41.0. Emits deprecation warning supplied.

title: Compare one watershed group against a reference dataset — lnk_compare_wsg
text: list two elements: rollup: tibble one row per (species, habitat_type) — 7 habitat types: spawning, rearing, lake_rearing, wetland_rearing, rearing_stream, rearing_lake_centerline, rearing_wetland_centerline. Columns: wsg, species, habitat_type, unit (km | ha), link_value, ref_value, diff_pct. mapping_code: tibble one row per species — segment-level match stats vs bcfishpass.streams_mapping_code. Columns: wsg, species, total_segs, match_pct, n_diffs, top_pattern, top_pattern_count. NULL mapping_code = FALSE.

title: Compare one watershed group against a reference dataset — lnk_compare_wsg
text: reference string identifying comparison source. Today "bcfishpass" supported (queries bcfishpass.habitat_linear_<sp> conn_ref). arg future-proofed default-bundle parity, regression detection across link runs, non-bcfp external data.

title: Compare one watershed group against a reference dataset — lnk_compare_wsg
text: mapping_code = FALSE mapping_code = TRUE run per-WSG pipeline . TRUE path purely additive — adds lnk_barriers_unify, lnk_barriers_views, lnk_pipeline_access, lnk_pipeline_mapping_code phases top network state, queries bcfishpass.streams_mapping_code segment- level diff. rollup tibble unchanged two modes. Side effects: writes per-WSG segment-level data persistent <persist_schema>.streams + streams_habitat_<sp> tables via lnk_pipeline_persist(). Drops <schema> working schema end unless cleanup_working = FALSE. Rollup methodology mirrors bcfp's habitat_linear_<sp> measures: linear km length_metre summed rearing/spawning-flagged segments, edge-type decomposition stream / lake-centerline / wetland-centerline slices. Lake / wetland area hectares uses DISTINCT waterbody_key joins whse_basemapping.fwa_lakes_poly / fwa_wetlands_poly avoid double-counting multi-segment lakes. See research/default_vs_bcfishpass.md measurement-asymmetry decision (link reports centerline km polygon ha; bcfp credits one depending species rule).

title: Compare one watershed group against a reference dataset — lnk_compare_wsg
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ conn <- lnk_db_conn() conn_ref <- DBI::dbConnect(RPostgres::Postgres(),   host = "localhost", port = 63333, dbname = "bcfishpass",   user = "newgraph", password = Sys.getenv("PG_PASS_SHARE")) cfg <- lnk_config("bcfishpass") loaded <- lnk_load_overrides(cfg)  # Rollup-only (~70s per WSG) result <- lnk_compare_wsg(   conn = conn, aoi = "ADMS",   cfg = cfg, loaded = loaded,   reference = "bcfishpass", conn_ref = conn_ref ) print(result$rollup)  # Add mapping_code lens (~100s per WSG) result_mc <- lnk_compare_wsg(   conn = conn, aoi = "ADMS",   cfg = cfg, loaded = loaded,   reference = "bcfishpass", conn_ref = conn_ref,   mapping_code = TRUE ) print(result_mc$mapping_code) } # }

what: Load a Pipeline Config Bundle (Manifest) — lnk_config
title: Load a Pipeline Config Bundle (Manifest) — lnk_config
text: Reads config bundle manifest (config.yaml) returns single list object describing pipeline variant — paths, file declarations, pipeline knobs, provenance — parsed data.

title: Load a Pipeline Config Bundle (Manifest) — lnk_config
code: lnk_config(name_or_path)

title: Load a Pipeline Config Bundle (Manifest) — lnk_config
text: name_or_path Character. Either bundled config name ("bcfishpass", "default") absolute path config directory. Bundled names resolve system.file("extdata", "configs", name, package = "link").

title: Load a Pipeline Config Bundle (Manifest) — lnk_config
text: lnk_config S3 list slots: name — config name manifest dir — absolute path config directory description — manifest's free-text description (NULL) rules — absolute path rules YAML (consumed fresh::frs_habitat_classify()) dimensions — absolute path dimensions CSV (input lnk_rules_build()) species — character vector species rules YAML classifies (parsed rules.yaml top-level keys) files — named list file declarations. entry list path (resolved absolute path) optionally source (free-text provenance label) canonical_schema ("<source>/<file_name>" — keys crate's registry dispatch ingest via crate::crt_ingest()) pipeline — named list pipeline knobs (apply_habitat_overlay, break_order, cluster, spawn_connected) provenance — named list per-file provenance metadata, keyed file path relative dir. Drift detection checksums lives lnk_config_verify(). extends — character NULL, parent config name/path manifest declared (post-resolution; used callers beyond audit)

title: Load a Pipeline Config Bundle (Manifest) — lnk_config
text: Tabular data (override CSVs, habitat classifications, parameters) loaded lnk_load_overrides(), dispatches declared file crate::crt_ingest() source-registered entries falls local reads otherwise. split keeps lnk_config() cheap call (CSV parsing) lets provenance-consumers like lnk_config_verify() lnk_stamp() work without touching data. config bundle directory inst/extdata/configs/<name>/ (bundled variants) arbitrary directory path (custom variants) containing config.yaml plus files manifest references. file paths manifest resolved relative bundle directory. Configs may declare extends: <parent> inherit another config. parent resolved (recursively, also extends) merged shallowly: child entries override parent entries key files:, pipeline:, provenance:; top-level scalars (description, rules, dimensions) override directly.

what: Verify Config Bundle File Checksums and Shape — lnk_config_verify
title: Verify Config Bundle File Checksums and Shape — lnk_config_verify
text: Recomputes sha256 byte shape checksums every file declared bundle's provenance: block compares recorded values. Returns tibble expected vs observed; flags drift axis separately.

title: Verify Config Bundle File Checksums and Shape — lnk_config_verify
code: lnk_config_verify(cfg, strict = FALSE)

title: Verify Config Bundle File Checksums and Shape — lnk_config_verify
text: cfg lnk_config object lnk_config(). strict Logical. TRUE, errors file drifted either axis. Default FALSE warns returns tibble inspection.

title: Verify Config Bundle File Checksums and Shape — lnk_config_verify
text: tibble columns: file — path relative cfg$dir byte_expected — byte checksum recorded manifest byte_observed — byte checksum recomputed current file byte_drift — logical, TRUE byte checksums differ shape_expected — shape checksum recorded manifest, NA manifest shape_checksum field shape_observed — shape checksum recomputed current file's header line shape_drift — logical, TRUE shape checksums differ (manifest shape_expected compare ) missing — logical, TRUE file longer exists disk (observed values NA) tibble carries one row per provenanced file. bundle provenance: block (cfg$provenance NULL) returns empty tibble columns.

title: Verify Config Bundle File Checksums and Shape — lnk_config_verify
text: Byte drift (byte_drift) — file content changed (rows added/edited/removed, whole-file re-shape). Detected via sha256 full file. Catches every kind change tell kind. Shape drift (shape_drift) — file's header changed (column added / renamed / removed / reshaped). Detected via sha256 first line file (whitespace-normalized). pure-value change (rows added column change) shows byte_drift = TRUE shape_drift = FALSE. column rename shows TRUE. Header-fingerprint catches dominant failure mode (column structure change); type changes within stable columns detected — require value-level inspection scope . Use run time detect silent drift — file edited without re-recording checksum, external CSV re-synced path. Drift two pipeline runs DB state package versions almost always traces back config-file edit; lnk_config_verify() fastest way localize change.

title: Load override CSVs into a database table — lnk_load
code: lnk_load(   conn,   csv,   to,   cols_id = "modelled_crossing_id",   cols_required = NULL,   cols_provenance = c("reviewer", "review_date", "source"),   overwrite = TRUE )

title: Load override CSVs into a database table — lnk_load
text: conn DBI::DBIConnection object. csv Character. Path CSV file, vector paths load multiple files table (e.g., one per field season). Character. Schema-qualified destination table name (e.g., "working.overrides_modelled"). cols_id Character. Column(s) used crossing identifier. System-agnostic — "stream_crossing_id", "chris_culvert_id", ID system uses. cols_required Character vector. Columns must exist every CSV. Fails fast informative error naming missing column file. cols_id always required need repeated . cols_provenance Character vector. Provenance columns track reviewed . Kept present CSV, silently skipped absent. Set NULL disable provenance tracking. overwrite Logical. TRUE (default), drop recreate table. FALSE, append existing table.

title: Load override CSVs into a database table — lnk_load
text: destination table name (invisibly), piping lnk_override_validate() lnk_override_apply().

title: Load override CSVs into a database table — lnk_load
text: Override CSVs represent hand-reviewed crossing corrections accumulated across field seasons imagery review. row says "crossing's attribute changed value." Fail fast: structure validated data written. Missing required columns produce clear error naming column file path. Multi-file load: pass vector CSV paths combine overrides different sources (field seasons, watersheds, reviewers). first file creates table; subsequent files append. Provenance optional: teams mature QA processes track reviewer, review_date, source. New projects can start without columns add later.

title: Build per-segment per-species mapping_code tokens from schema tables — lnk_mapping_code
text: conn DBI::DBIConnection local pipeline DB. table_access Character. Schema-qualified name streams_access table (e.g. "working_pars.streams_access" "fresh_default.streams_access"). table_habitat Character. Schema-qualified name long-form habitat source — either working-schema streams_habitat table persist streams_habitat_long_vw view. Must columns id_segment, watershed_group_code, species_code, spawning, rearing. table_streams Character. Schema-qualified name streams table, queried id_segment + feature_code. aoi Character. Watershed group code (e.g. "PARS") — filters input queries one WSG. table_to Character NULL. Optional schema-qualified destination table result. non-NULL, lnk_pipeline_mapping_code() writes tibble via dbWriteTable(overwrite = TRUE). Default NULL — returns-. presence Named logical vector NULL. Per-species presence flag aoi. NULL function derives presence data: species present iff least one habitat row spawning = TRUE rearing = TRUE. Pass explicit values override (e.g. force-include species QGIS symbology even segments accessible). species_resident Character. Species using resident flavor mapping_code_barrier. Default c("bt", "wct"). Pass-lnk_pipeline_mapping_code()'s resident_species arg. species_anadromous Character. Species using anadromous flavor. Default c("ch", "cm", "co", "pk", "sk", "st"). Pass-lnk_pipeline_mapping_code()'s anadromous_species arg. species_spawn_only Character. Species without rearing semantics. Default c("cm", "pk"). Pass-lnk_pipeline_mapping_code()'s spawn_only_species arg.

title: Annotate a parity rollup against the bcfp divergence taxonomy — lnk_parity_annotate
text: Joins row parity rollup (lnk_compare_wsg() data-raw/compare_bcfishpass_wsg.R wrapper) first taxonomy entry whose wsg, species, metric, pattern, (optional) diff_range match. Unmatched rows |diff_pct| >= tolerance tagged class = UNEXPLAINED, status = NEEDS_INVESTIGATION; smaller residuals become class = WITHIN_TOLERANCE, status = CLOSED. Rows NA diff_pct (NA ref value, divide--zero) become class = NOT_APPLICABLE.

code: lnk_pipeline_access(   conn,   segments,   aoi,   to = NULL,   barriers_per_sp = list(),   observations = NULL,   wsg_presence = list(),   presence = NULL,   barrier_sources = list(),   crossings_table = NULL,   segment_id_col = "id_segment" )

title: Build per-segment access codes + downstream-feature arrays — lnk_pipeline_access
text: conn DBI::DBIConnection object pointing fwapg. segments Character. Schema-qualified segments table. aoi Character. Watershed group code (e.g. "ADMS"). Filter applied segments via watershed_group_code = aoi. Character NULL. Optional schema-qualified output table. supplied, wide streams_access shape written via dbWriteTable(overwrite = TRUE); either case tibble returned invisibly. Default NULL returns-. barriers_per_sp Named list. name species code (e.g. "bt"); value schema-qualified barriers table species (e.g. "working_adms.barriers_bt"). barriers table must <sp>_id column (e.g. barriers_bt_id) plus standard (blue_line_key, downstream_route_measure, wscode_ltree, localcode_ltree) keys. observations Character NULL. Optional schema-qualified observations table (observation_key, species_code, ...) + standard FWA keys. provided, drives access code distinction 1 (modelled) 2 (observed). Default NULL collapses observation-distinguishing logic — every accessible segment gets access_<sp> = 1. wsg_presence Named logical. One per species (matching barriers_per_sp keys), TRUE species present aoi. Sets access_<sp> = -9 species marked FALSE. Default empty list assumes species present (-9 codes emitted). barrier_sources Named list. name arbitrary source tag (e.g. "anthropogenic", "pscis", "dams", "remediations"); value schema-qualified barriers table source. Output gains one has_barriers_<source>_dnstr boolean column per source. Unlike barriers_per_sp, sources drive species access integer code – bcfp-shape dnstr indicators consumed lnk_pipeline_mapping_code(). Optional; default empty. "anthropogenic" "dams" present, output gains dam_dnstr_ind boolean column: TRUE iff next- downstream anthropogenic barrier also dam (sequence-aware, mirrors bcfp's array[barriers_anthropogenic_dnstr[1]] && barriers_dams_dnstr SQL). Required resident-flavor mapping_code_bt / mapping_code_wct parity bcfp. "remediations" present crossings_table set, output gains remediated_dnstr_ind boolean column. crossings_table Character NULL. Schema-qualified crossings table aggregated_crossings_id pscis_status columns (e.g. "bcfishpass.crossings"). Used compute remediated_dnstr_ind (TRUE iff next-downstream remediation crossing whose pscis_status ('REMEDIATED', 'PASSABLE')). bcfp's streams_access.remediated_dnstr_ind currently buggy (see smnorris/bcfishpass#690): JOIN clause pscis_status = 'REMEDIATED' pscis_status = 'PASSABLE' contradictory always FALSE – verified 4.2M rows. link computes bcfp-intended semantics, link's mapping_code may emit REMEDIATED tokens segments bcfp's current output emits DAM / MODELLED / ASSESSED. PR filed NewGraphEnvironment/bcfishpass fork; lands + propagates upstream outputs converge. Default NULL skips remediated_dnstr_ind column. segment_id_col Character. Default "id_segment".

title: Build per-segment per-species mapping_code strings (bcfp parity) — lnk_pipeline_mapping_code
text: Vocabulary (per species): Token 1 (habitat) per bcfp: ACCESS: species' barriers upstream (.e. accessible species) segment spawning rearing eligibility species SPAWN: spawning eligibility > 0 (always wins REAR) REAR: rearing eligibility > 0 spawning "" (empty): species least one downstream barrier blocking (habitat label suppressed – inaccessible). species without rearing semantics (CM, PK), rearing conditions drop – ACCESS (spawn, barriers) SPAWN (spawning > 0) emit. Token 2 (barrier source) emits species' barriers downstream empty (.e. accessible). Resident-flavor (BT, WCT) anadromous-flavor (CH/CM/CO/PK/SK/ST) differ CASE order: Resident:    REMEDIATED > DAM > ASSESSED (anthropogenic + pscis + dam) > MODELLED (anthropogenic + pscis + dam) > NONE (anthropogenic). Anadromous:  REMEDIATED > DAM (dam) > ASSESSED (pscis) > MODELLED (anthropogenic) > NONE. Token 3 emits INTERMITTENT segment's feature_code matches bcfp intermittent code (default "GA24850150") species' barriers downstream empty. Empty / NULL tokens dropped via paste(..., collapse = ";")-style composition inaccessible segment yields "" (empty string) accessible -habitat--intermittent segment yields "ACCESS;NONE".
code: {ACCESS|SPAWN|REAR|""} ; {NONE|DAM|MODELLED|ASSESSED|REMEDIATED} [;INTERMITTENT]

Existing table DROP TABLE EXISTS'd first. geom_col Name geometry column table_in. Default "geom". snap_tolerance Maximum snap distance metres. Points farther network dropped. Default 100. exclude_edge_types Integer vector edge_type codes exclude FWA network snapping. Default 1425L (subsurface flow). Pass integer(0) exclude none. blue_line_key_col Optional name blue_line_key column table_in constrain candidate streams . NULL (default) snaps FWA stream within tolerance. stream_order_min Optional minimum stream_order include. NULL (default) accepts order. num_features Integer scalar. Maximum number stream candidates per input point. Default 1L (nearest-— backwards compatible). Set higher (e.g. 5L) downstream scoring/dedup workflows need multiple candidates per point (e.g. bcfp PSCIS--stream selection stream-name match disambiguates among nearby streams). Output one row per (input row, candidate stream) pair, ordered distance ascending.

title: Snap a Postgres table of points to the FWA stream network — lnk_points_snap

title: Snap a Postgres table of points to the FWA stream network — lnk_points_snap
text: Uses single SQL CROSS JOIN LATERAL ... ORDER <-> ... LIMIT 1 whse_basemapping.fwa_stream_networks_sp — lateral-KNN pattern bcfishpass's load_dams.sql link's existing CABD dams snap lnk_pipeline_prepare(). One round-trip; scales province-wide point sets. Generic — specific pipeline phase. Likely belongs future pac package scaffolded; ships link now. Output table columns: every column table_in PLUS linear_feature_id (bigint), blue_line_key (integer), downstream_route_measure (numeric), wscode_ltree (ltree), localcode_ltree (ltree), distance_to_stream (numeric, metres), geom_snapped (geometry — point projected onto segment). Filters applied candidate streams (fresh::frs_point_snap_knn conventions): wscode_ltree != '999' (placeholder streams excluded) localcode_ltree NULL (unmapped tributaries excluded) edge_type (exclude_edge_types) (subsurface etc. excluded)

title: Produce a fresh-compatible break source list — lnk_source
code: # --- The link -> fresh handoff --- if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ conn <- lnk_db_conn()  # Step 1: Score crossings with link lnk_score_severity(conn, "working.crossings")  # Step 2: Produce break source src <- lnk_source(conn, "working.crossings") # Returns: # list(table = "working.crossings", #      label_col = "severity", #      label_map = c(high = "blocked", moderate = "potential"))  # Step 3: Feed to fresh — link's output is fresh's input frs_habitat(conn, "BULK", break_sources = list(src))  # --- Combine with other break sources --- frs_habitat(conn, "BULK", break_sources = list(   src,   list(table = "working.falls", label = "blocked"),   list(table = "working.dams", label = "blocked"))) # link scored crossings + falls + dams — all as break sources.  # --- Custom label_map for a conservative project --- # Only treat high-severity as blocked src_strict <- lnk_source(conn, "working.crossings",   label_map = c(high = "blocked"))  # --- Static label for all crossings --- src_all <- lnk_source(conn, "working.crossings",   label = "potential", label_col = NULL) # Every crossing is a potential barrier — no severity differentiation. } # }

title: Load configurable severity scoring thresholds — lnk_thresholds
text: csv Path CSV file columns severity, metric, value. provided, CSV values override -code defaults. Use system.file("extdata", "thresholds_default.csv", package = "link") see expected format. high Named list metric thresholds high severity. Merged CSV values (inline wins conflict). moderate Named list metric thresholds moderate severity. low Named list metric thresholds low severity.

title: Load configurable severity scoring thresholds — lnk_thresholds
text: named list elements high, moderate, low, containing named list metric thresholds (numeric values keyed metric name).

title: Load configurable severity scoring thresholds — lnk_thresholds
text: Thresholds control lnk_score_severity() classifies crossings. default values reflect BC provincial fish passage assessment criteria: High severity outlet_drop >= 0.6m slope_length >= 120 — impassable species flows Moderate severity outlet_drop >= 0.3m slope_length >= 60 — flow-dependent, potentially passable migration flows Low severity everything else crossing present System-agnostic: metric names user-defined strings, hardcoded provincial data system. New Zealand user might define high = list(perch_height = 0.5, pipe_gradient = 0.05). Merge order: code defaults < CSV values < inline arguments. lets ship project CSV still tweak one threshold inline.

title: Load configurable severity scoring thresholds — lnk_thresholds
code: # Default BC thresholds — zero config th <- lnk_thresholds() th$high$outlet_drop #> [1] 0.6 # [1] 0.6  # Override from bundled CSV (same result, shows the format) csv_path <- system.file("extdata", "thresholds_default.csv", package = "link") th_csv <- lnk_thresholds(csv = csv_path) identical(th, th_csv) #> [1] TRUE  # Project-specific: bull trout tolerate higher drops th_bt <- lnk_thresholds(high = list(outlet_drop = 0.8)) th_bt$high$outlet_drop #> [1] 0.8 # [1] 0.8 — inline override wins  # How thresholds plug into scoring (the integration point) if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ conn <- lnk_db_conn() lnk_score_severity(conn, "working.crossings",   thresholds = lnk_thresholds(high = list(outlet_drop = 0.8))) } # }

title: link 0.40.5
text: Tunnel-free per-segment mapping_code parity 3 FWCP study areas (#175) — 50 drainage-closed WSGs across Peace / Fraser / Skeena, authoritative median match 99.66% / mean 99.11% / 130 148 rows ≥99%. Built around new lnk_access() export (#205) — portable access builder ’s missing twin lnk_mapping_code. merge = TRUE mode cheap post-consolidate recompute: rebuild access + mapping_code persisted streams/habitat/barriers (streams segmentation habitat classification re-derived), ~8× faster full-pipeline path (FINA 11.9 s wall vs ~90 s, identical bcfp parity). Methodology now correctness-regardless--bucketing — distribute (bucketing) → consolidate → recompute → compare — recompute correctness guarantee, bucketing speed knob. New runner data-raw/study_area_run.sh + helpers (study_area_wsgs.R, wsg_run_one.R, wsg_recompute_one.R, study_area_compare.R) drives whole flow tunnel-free M1 + N cyphers (:63333, M4). Bug classes addressed along way: persist + consolidate now host- species-count-agnostic (#204 — shape-tolerant schema_consolidate + cypher_prep aligned cfg$species); lnk_mapping_code’s access read longer goes cartesian persist id_segment repeats across WSGs (#203 symptom narrowed — filter watershed_group_code present); persist streams + barriers get wscode_ltree / localcode_ltree GIST/btree indexes frs_network_features traversal needs (matches fresh::utils.R pattern). New RUNBOOK.md §6 gotchas (orphaned frs_network_features backends + statement_timeout / lock_timeout, view-vs-table planner direction, per-tenant cartesian) cross-repo soul code-check.md Postgres + R-client section. Two recompute-stable divergences remain taxonomy candidates, regressions: SETN salmon ~94% (SK-geography class), UNRS BT 61.8% (Kenney reservoir / dam-override). Validated bcfishpass@v0.7.15-14-ge12c1a5.

title: link 0.40.4
text: Reproduce bcfp’s per-species accessibility dam-downstream segments emit dam descriptor (#200). mapping_code phase previously drove accessible barriers_<sp>_unified (barriers, including dams), every segment dam read inaccessible lost ;DAM/;MODELLED/;ASSESSED second token — emitting bare SPAWN/REAR bcfp emits SPAWN;DAM. now uses new per-species barriers_<sp>_access view reproduces bcfp’s barriers_<sp>: natural barriers (gradient species threshold ∪ falls ∪ subsurface), minus observation/habitat override, plus user-definite barriers (override-exempt). Dams stay barrier_sources annotate token2 . three access inputs now persisted province-wide cross-WSG downstream walk correct every watershed group, just run’s : natural barriers (already), user_barriers_definite (new USER_DEFINITE family lnk_barriers_unify, ltree-resolved via FWA join like falls), observation/habitat override (new <persist_schema>.barrier_overrides table). Validated bcfishpass@v0.7.15: PARS BT 98.95%, LFRA BT 97.77% / CO 97.90% per-segment mapping_code match. See RUNBOOK.md §5.

title: link 0.40.3
text: Persist per-source downstream-barrier flag columns streams_access lnk_pipeline_mapping_code’s second token (DAM/MODELLED/ASSESSED/REMEDIATED/NONE) populates persisted state instead defaulting NONE. Three coupled fixes (#196): lnk_persist_init adds six flag columns streams_access DDL; lnk_pipeline_run pre-persists barriers mapping_code phase cross-WSG dam visibility (link#152); lnk_pipeline_persist projects flag columns INSERT (DDL/INSERT pair must match — missing projection actual NONE-token bug). Adds RUNBOOK.md — durable mental model barrier → access → mapping_code machinery, including authoritative bcfp access-set mechanism (read smnorris/bcfishpass@v0.7.15). Note: per-species accessibility set still carries dams (natural-+ observation/habitat-overridden, per bcfp); dam-downstream segments therefore still emit bare habitat token rather SPAWN;DAM. Characterized RUNBOOK §5 scoped fix (follow-issue).

title: link 0.40.2
text: Hotfix wide-table species-set evolution v0.40.0/v0.40.1’s lnk_pipeline_run(mapping_code = TRUE) path. Closes #194. v0.40.1 made mapping_code phase use active_species (per-WSG subset bundle species) working schema’s streams_access columns — matching persist DDL lnk_persist_init also passed active_species. persist province-wide. WSG #2 different active subset WSG #1, persist table locked #1’s column set #2’s INSERT projection fails: Live smoke 2026-05-19: PARS ran first (default config, active = bt/gr/ko/rb) → 4-column persist DDL. title: link 0.40.0
text: Mapping_code tunnel decouple + portable lnk_mapping_code() build + <type>_<role> rename sweep. Closes #187. Major architectural shift access semantics flow parity diff. BC: parameter CLI-flag renames (deprecation shims one release; removal v0.41.0). Persist streams_access + streams_mapping_code + long-form habitat view. lnk_persist_init() now creates two new per-WSG per-species persist tables (streams_access, streams_mapping_code) one VIEW (streams_habitat_long_vw = UNION across streams_habitat_<sp> tables, presents per-species split long-form consumer prefers ). Per-species column generators (.lnk_cols_streams_access_per_sp(), .lnk_cols_streams_mapping_code_per_sp()) species-driven — pass different species set, get matching columns. lnk_pipeline_persist() extended streams_access + streams_mapping_code write blocks, gated presence working-side tables (skip cleanly mapping_code path didn’t run). lnk_mapping_code() — new exported portable build entry point. Schema-aware wrapper around lnk_pipeline_mapping_code() (pure data transform). Takes explicit table_<role> args (table_access, table_habitat, table_streams) — function works working-schema tables (mid-pipeline) persist-schema tables (ad-hoc rebuild). Caller can invoke directly persist data tunnel rebuild streams_mapping_code without re-running full pipeline — headline use case unblocking QGIS bcfp-shape symbology via data-raw/build_species_views.R --bcfp. lnk_pipeline_run(..., mapping_code = TRUE) — tunnel-free mapping_code phase. New optional phase runs lnk_barriers_views (working <schema>.barriers, tunnel-free, link-canonical) + lnk_pipeline_access + lnk_mapping_code lnk_barriers_unify lnk_pipeline_persist.
